creator: eduardo@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Erik Jensen from comment #30)

Yes, I was writing this at same time:

---
-std=c++14 is set auto on 140, 131 and 124 with clang 14.0.5 on 140 and 13.0.0 on 131 and 124 as we can see at https://people.freebsd.org/~eduardo/logs/root/v3/

(using -DCMAKE_CXX_STANDARD=17 will automatically enable ROOT7 still experimental, so best bet is using 14 as ROOT7 will build more things)

So this behaviour could be due to different compiler versions not standards.

What should we do?

- forcing same clang version to be used in different bsd versions with USES=   llvm:13 could be excessive since we don't getting build problems

- using an exception with an if condition to add/remove "std_config.pcm" file from plist?(In reply to Erik Jensen from comment #31)
---
